sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Frontier Group Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:ULCC) Given Average Rating of "Hold" by Brokerages
feed.summary: Frontier Group Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:ULCC) has received an average "Hold" rating from brokerages, with an average 12-month price target of $6.38. The company recently reported quarterly EPS of ($0.34) which matched estimates, and revenue of $886 million, below expectations. Insider selling has been noted recently, although insiders still own a significant portion of the company's stock.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII) After A 113% One Year Surge?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII) after its 113% surge over the past year, using Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) and Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio to assess its valuation. While the DCF model suggests HII is fairly valued, trading at an approximate 8.4% discount to its intrinsic value, the P/E ratio indicates it is slightly overvalued compared to its proprietary Fair Ratio. The article provides context on investor interest in defense and shipbuilding and encourages readers to explore further analysis on Simply Wall St.

feed.title: Dollar General (DG) Valuation Check After Sharply Divergent Short And Long Term Shareholder Returns
feed.summary: Dollar General (DG) has seen a significant rebound in its share price over the last three months, contrasting with weaker long-term performance. While analyst narratives suggest the stock is overvalued at US$151.73 compared to a fair value of US$122.68, Simply Wall St's DCF model indicates potential underpricing with a fair value of US$173.17. The article encourages investors to analyze the underlying assumptions and risks, and to consider building their own narrative based on their assessment of growth and value.

feed.title: Chemours Sells Taiwan Titanium Site for $360 Million Cash
feed.summary: Chemours has agreed to sell its former titanium dioxide manufacturing site in Taiwan for approximately $360 million in cash. The proceeds will be used to reduce company debt, enhancing liquidity and financial stability. The transaction is slated to close by mid-2026, contingent on regulatory approval, allowing Chemours to focus on its core business areas and optimize its market strategy.

feed.title: Applied Digital Appoints Co-Founder Jason Zhang as President
feed.summary: Applied Digital (NASDAQ: APLD) has appointed co-founder and current Chief Strategy Officer, Jason Zhang, as the company’s President. This appointment formalizes Zhang’s significant leadership role in driving the company's innovation and strategic direction since its inception. He will continue to partner with Chairman and CEO Wes Cummins to scale the business and advance its AI infrastructure growth.

feed.title: Dorman products director Steven L. Berman sells $827,477 in shares
feed.summary: This article reports on Dorman Products director Steven L. Berman selling 9,737 shares of the company's stock on January 10, 2024, at an average price of $85.09 per share, totaling $827,477. Following the transaction, Berman still directly owns 20,443 shares of Dorman Products, valued at approximately $1,739,598.

feed.title: Bluerock Homes Trust (BHM) Announces First Quarter Dividends on Series A Preferred Stock and Series B Preferred Stock
feed.summary: Bluerock Homes Trust (NYSE American: BHM) has declared monthly cash dividends for Q1 2026 on its Series A and Series B redeemable preferred stocks. The Series A preferred stock will receive a quarterly rate of $0.375 per share, including enhanced special dividends tied to the average one-month SOFR plus 2.0%. The Series B preferred stock will receive a quarterly rate of $0.46875 per share, with both series having set payment and record dates in February, March, and April 2026.

feed.title: Emergency trauma gear brand My Medic bought for $18.7M by Acme United
feed.summary: Acme United Corporation (NYSE American: ACU) has acquired the assets of My Medic, a supplier of tactical, trauma, and emergency response products, for $18.7 million. My Medic, which generated approximately $19 million in revenue in 2025 and boasts over 500,000 social media followers, will expand Acme United's direct-to-consumer presence. The acquisition includes partial an earnout contingent on future revenue and an indemnity holdback.

feed.title: Halper Sadeh Investigates Dentsply Sirona Executives for Breach of Fiduciary Duties
feed.summary: Halper Sadeh LLC is investigating Dentsply Sirona Inc. executives for potential breach of fiduciary duties to shareholders, focusing on corporate governance and shareholder rights. The firm offers to represent long-term shareholders on a contingency fee basis to seek reforms and financial recovery. Shareholder participation is encouraged to enhance company transparency and oversight.

feed.title: Voya Equity Closed End Funds Declare Distributions
feed.summary: Voya Investment Management has announced the January distributions for five of its closed-end funds: IGA, IGD, IDE, IAE, and IHD. These distributions will be paid on February 17, 2026, to shareholders of record on February 2, 2026, with individual per-share amounts ranging from $0.050 to $0.100. The announcement also detailed the estimated sources of these distributions and provided performance data based on net asset value for the funds.

feed.title: Cresud issues $87.6 million in notes in Argentine capital markets
feed.summary: Cresud Sociedad Anónima Comercial, Inmobiliaria, Financiera y Agropecuaria (NASDAQ:CRESY) has issued $87.6 million in notes in the Argentine local capital markets. The issuance consists of two tranches, with varied interest rates, maturity dates, and settlement terms, all denominated in U.S. dollars and following a bullet amortization structure.

feed.title: Bid to strengthen financial collaboration by Bank of Montreal
feed.summary: Bank of Montreal (BMO), Canada's oldest bank, is committed to strengthening its role as a financial bridge between China and North America, leveraging its historical ties and cross-border expertise. With a presence in China dating back to 1983 and local incorporation in 2010, BMO offers a comprehensive suite of services and holds strategic equity stakes in Chinese financial institutions like Fullgoal Fund Management and COFCO Trust. BMO aims to facilitate trade and investment between the two regions, support Chinese companies expanding abroad, and assist North American companies entering China, while also aiding Chinese families and students in Canada.

feed.title: Prudential Financial's Japan life unit CEO to resign after widespread staff misconduct
feed.summary: The CEO of Prudential Financial's Japan life insurance unit, Kan Mabara, will resign effective February 1, 2026, following revelations of widespread misconduct by about 100 employees. The misconduct involved approximately 3.1 billion yen ($19.60 million) and affected 498 customers through activities like embezzlement, improper receipt of funds, and personal loans from customers. The unit commenced a review in August 2024 after discovering multiple similar cases among current and former employees.

feed.title: Brookfield Renewable Partners L.P. (NYSE:BEP) Receives Consensus Rating of "Moderate Buy" from Analysts
feed.summary: Brookfield Renewable Partners L.P. (NYSE:BEP) has received a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating from sixteen brokerages, with an average 1-year price target of $34.86. Despite a negative payout ratio indicating distributions exceed reported earnings, the company reported a smaller-than-expected loss per share and has seen significant institutional investment. BEP pays a quarterly dividend of $0.373, yielding around 5.3%, and its stock is currently trading around $28.08.

feed.title: BioNTech’s Oncology Ambition Gains Momentum with Key Regulatory Designation
feed.summary: BioNTech is strategically shifting its focus from its COVID-19 vaccine success to becoming a leader in oncology, a pivot validated by the FDA granting Orphan Drug designation to its therapeutic candidate, gotistobart, for lung cancer. The company aims to expand its Phase 3 oncology clinical trials to 15 by late 2026 and expects to report data from seven late-stage studies this year, despite anticipating declining Comirnaty revenue and no significant oncology revenue in the current year. With substantial liquid assets and upcoming financial results in March 2026, BioNTech faces continued market skepticism but is pursuing ambitious growth in its cancer treatment pipeline.

feed.title: Wall Street Analysts Think Commvault (CVLT) Is a Good Investment: Is It?
feed.summary: This article examines Wall Street analysts' recommendations for Commvault (CVLT), noting its Average Brokerage Recommendation (ABR) of 1.43, indicating a "Strong Buy" to "Buy" consensus. However, it cautions against relying solely on ABR due to inherent analyst bias and suggests cross-referencing with the Zacks Rank, which for CVLT is currently a "Hold" (#3), based on earnings estimate revisions. The article highlights that while brokerage firm recommendations often have a positive bias, the Zacks Rank is a more reliable indicator of near-term price performance driven by earnings estimate revisions.

feed.title: Agilon Health, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit Reminder for Investors
feed.summary: Agilon Health, Inc. (NYSE: AGL) is facing a securities fraud lawsuit, with a lead plaintiff motion deadline of March 2, 2026. The lawsuit stems from a significant stock price plunge in August 2025—a 51.5% drop—following the departure of its President and CEO, disappointing earnings, and suspended guidance. Allegations include materially false and misleading statements made by the company during the class period, which ran from February 26 to August 4, 2025.

feed.title: ASML Stock Upgraded by KGI Securities to 'Outperform' with New P
feed.summary: KGI Securities has upgraded ASML Holding (ASML) stock from 'Hold' to 'Outperform' and set a new price target of $1,415.00 USD. This upgrade follows similar positive revisions by other analysts recently, suggesting strong growth potential for the semiconductor lithography leader. Despite an average analyst target price indicating a potential downside from its current price, the consensus brokerage recommendation for ASML remains "Outperform."

feed.title: Lockheed delivered record 191 F-35s as it cleared out TR-3 backlog
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin delivered a record 191 F-35 Joint Strike Fighters in 2025, significantly surpassing previous delivery numbers after resolving issues with the Technology Refresh 3 (TR-3) upgrade. This record delivery cleared a backlog of jets that had accumulated due to a government-imposed halt on deliveries. The TR-3 upgrades were crucial for future modernizations and enabled F-35s to play critical roles in combat operations, including suppressing Iran’s air defenses and engaging Russian drones.

feed.title: Analog Devices (ADI) Surpasses Market Returns: Some Facts Worth Knowing
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) stock closed at $302.10, outperforming the S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq with a 1.38% gain. Over the past month, ADI has climbed 9.94%, significantly exceeding its sector and the S&P 500. The company anticipates strong year-over-year growth in its upcoming earnings report, with projected EPS of $2.29 and revenue of $3.11 billion, and currently holds a Zacks Rank of #1 (Strong Buy).

feed.title: Teradyne (TER) Stock Drops Despite Market Gains: Important Facts to Note
feed.summary: Despite overall market gains, Teradyne (TER) stock dropped by 1.08% in the recent trading session. The company is set to announce its earnings on February 2, 2026, with analysts projecting significant increases in EPS and revenue. Teradyne currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) and is trading at a premium compared to its industry's average Forward P/E ratio.

feed.title: Is Linde plc the Quiet Cash Beast Wall Street Loves But TikTok Sleeps On?
feed.summary: This article examines Linde plc (LIN), an industrial gas giant, arguing that it is a strong long-term investment despite lacking social media hype. It highlights Linde's essential role across various industries, its involvement in clean energy and hydrogen, and its consistent financial performance with dividends. The article compares Linde favorably against competitors like Air Products and Chemicals, positioning it as a stable, growth-oriented stock for serious investors.

feed.title: A Look At CoStar Group (CSGP) Valuation After Its 2026 Outlook And Homes.com Profitability Delay
feed.summary: CoStar Group (CSGP) is under investor scrutiny after releasing its 2026 outlook, which detailed revenue and earnings guidance but delayed profitability for Homes.com. Despite a recent share price decline, the company aims for future growth through ongoing investment and a US$1.5 billion buyback plan. While one valuation narrative suggests the stock is 25.3% undervalued at US$84.82 per share due to expected rapid earnings expansion and AI integration, another perspective finds its current price-to-sales ratio to be a premium, suggesting it might be fully valued.

feed.title: What Procter & Gamble (PG)'s Leadership Shake-Up and Cost Cuts Mean For Shareholders
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble (PG) has announced a quarterly dividend of US$1.0568 per share, a new CEO (Shailesh Jejurikar), plans to cut 7,000 office jobs, and ongoing operational changes. These initiatives aim to address sales pressures and cost headwinds, while continuing to prioritize shareholder returns. The company's investment narrative is focused on its brands' ability to generate cash flow, with cost-cutting and innovation serving as short-term catalysts against risks like tariff-driven inflation.

feed.title: Assessing Realty Income (O) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum
feed.summary: Realty Income (O) has seen recent share price momentum, prompting investors to re-evaluate its long-term income profile. The stock is currently trading at $60.72, with one narrative suggesting a fair value of $61.26, indicating it's slightly undervalued especially considering its dividend stream. However, its P/E ratio of 58.1x, compared to the industry average of 27.7x, suggests potential valuation risk if market sentiment shifts.

feed.title: Check Point Software Technologies (CHKP) Valuation After China Restrictions And Renewed Analyst Optimism
feed.summary: Check Point Software Technologies (CHKP) is under scrutiny following China's restrictions on foreign cybersecurity tools, despite strong longer-term shareholder returns. While a popular narrative suggests CHKP is 16.8% undervalued at $226.51 due to billing strength and AI security expansion, Simply Wall St's DCF model indicates it is overvalued at its current price of $188.54, with a fair value of $165.68. Investors are encouraged to consider these differing valuations and potential risks when evaluating CHKP.

feed.title: Turner to Retire from Regions Financial, Chadha to Serve as CFO
feed.summary: David Turner, CFO of Regions Financial, will retire on March 31 after 20 years with the bank. Anil Chadha, currently controller and head of corporate finance, will succeed him as CFO. Chadha will oversee all finance operations and was praised by Turner and CEO John Turner for his expertise and strategic vision, ensuring a smooth transition for the company.

feed.title: Edison International Stock: Quiet Climb, Rising Expectations And A Power-Grid Reality Check
feed.summary: Edison International (EIX) has been experiencing a steady upward trend despite broader market wobbles in the utilities sector, driven by grid modernization efforts, wildfire risk management, and improving Wall Street sentiment. The stock's performance reflects investor confidence in its stability, dividend, and exposure to California's massive grid transformation. While the company faces ongoing challenges like regulatory risks and interest rate fluctuations, analysts are increasingly positive, viewing Edison International as an investable utility with a visible return profile.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges DeFi Technologies, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against DeFi Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: DEFT) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between May 12, 2025, and November 14, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that DeFi Technologies made false or misleading statements regarding delays in its DeFi arbitrage strategy, competition from other digital asset treasury companies, and its ability to meet revenue guidance for fiscal year 2025. Investors are encouraged to join the case, with a lead plaintiff deadline of January 30, 2026.

feed.title: Acadia Healthcare (ACHC) Is Down 14.4% After New York Medicaid Shift Tightens Out-of-State Reimbursement
feed.summary: Acadia Healthcare (ACHC) experienced a 14.4% drop after a New York Medicaid policy change tightened reimbursement for out-of-state behavioral health care, creating a US$25 million to US$30 million EBITDA headwind. Despite this, Acadia reiterated its 2025 revenue guidance of US$3.28 billion to US$3.30 billion, suggesting a contained immediate top-line impact. This highlights the company's sensitivity to state-level Medicaid decisions and concerns about future earnings and investor confidence.

feed.title: 60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals (SXTP) to Implement 1-for-4 Reverse Stock Split Effective January 20, 2026
feed.summary: 60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals (SXTP) will execute a 1-for-4 reverse stock split on January 20, 2026, to meet Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement. This will reduce outstanding shares and is expected to increase the stock price per share, with shares already rising 10% in premarket trading. Despite missing EPS expectations, the company's revenue exceeded forecasts, and analysts project a rise in stock price over the next 12 months.

feed.title: ALX Oncology Spotlights Evorpacept HER2 Strategy and EGFR ADC ALX2004 Progress at JPM Conference
feed.summary: ALX Oncology updated on its clinical-stage programs, evorpacept and ALX2004, at the J.P. Morgan conference. The company highlighted strong Aspen-06 subset results for evorpacept in HER2-retained gastric cancer, prompting a focus on HER2-positive breast cancer and multiple myeloma. ALX2004, an EGFR ADC, showed favorable preclinical data and is in Phase 1 dose escalation across multiple tumor types.

feed.title: Moderna Stock on the Move: What Sparked the 13% Jump in the Past Week?
feed.summary: Moderna's stock jumped 13% after the company announced strong preliminary sales for 2025 and a positive business outlook for 2026 and beyond, including revised operating expense forecasts and an increased cash position. The company also detailed anticipated milestones for its pipeline, such as regulatory filings for its seasonal influenza vaccine mRNA-1010 and progress on its personalized cancer therapy, intismeran autogene, developed with Merck. These updates indicate a promising future, with a target of achieving break-even on an operating cash flow basis by 2028.

feed.title: Expedia Group to Webcast Fourth Quarter 2025 Results on February 12, 2026
feed.summary: Expedia Group announced it will report its fourth quarter 2025 financial results on Thursday, February 12, 2026. The results will be available via an earnings release and webcast on the company's Investor Relations website after market close, with the webcast beginning at 1:30 PM Pacific Time / 4:30 PM Eastern Time. A replay of the call will be accessible for approximately twelve months.

feed.title: Brink’s Declares Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: The Brink's Company (NYSE:BCO) announced that its board of directors declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.255 cents per share on the company's common stock. This dividend is scheduled for payment on March 2, 2026, to shareholders who are on record as of February 2, 2026. Brink's is a global provider of cash and valuables management, digital retail solutions, and ATM managed services.

feed.title: East West Bancorp (EWBC) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release
feed.summary: East West Bancorp (EWBC) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. The company's upcoming earnings report on January 22 could see its stock price move based on how actual results compare to estimates. With a positive Zacks Earnings ESP and a Zacks Rank #3, East West Bancorp is likely to beat the consensus EPS estimate.

feed.title: CASI Pharmaceuticals Announces Approval of Clinical Trial Application by China NMPA for Phase 1 / 2 Study of CID-103 for Renal Allograft Antibody-Mediated Rejection (AMR)
feed.summary: CASI Pharmaceuticals has announced that the China NMPA has approved its Clinical Trial Application for a Phase 1/2 study of CID-103 for renal allograft antibody-mediated rejection (AMR). CID-103 is an anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody intended to treat AMR, a condition with no currently approved therapies that often leads to kidney transplant loss. This approval, combined with a previous U.S. FDA IND approval for a Phase 1 AMR study, marks significant progress in the company's efforts to develop a treatment for this critical unmet medical need.

feed.title: September 18th Options Now Available For Canadian Imperial Bank Of Commerce (CM)
feed.summary: New options contracts for Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce (CM) with a September 18th expiration date are now available. These contracts offer potential opportunities for option sellers to achieve higher premiums due to the extended time until expiration. Stock Options Channel's analysis highlights a particular put contract at a $90.00 strike and a call contract at a $92.50 strike, suggesting potential YieldBoost returns for investors.

feed.title: Tilray Brands (NASDAQ:TLRY) Shares Down 1.9% - What's Next?
feed.summary: Tilray Brands (NASDAQ:TLRY) shares fell 1.9% during mid-day trading to $9.71, with trading volume down 41% compared to the average session. The stock currently hovers near its 50-day moving average and below its 200-day average, holding a "Reduce" consensus rating and a $15 target price from analysts. Tilray missed quarterly EPS estimates at ($0.41) against an expected ($0.14) but exceeded revenue expectations with $217.5 million, displaying significant negative net margins and return on equity.

feed.title: Worthington Steel to buy metal processor Kloeckner in deal valued at $2.4B (WS:NYSE)
feed.summary: Worthington Steel (WS) plans to acquire metal processing firm Kloeckner & Co. in a $2.4 billion deal, which will create the second-largest steel service center company in North America. Following the announcement, Worthington Steel's stock rose 3.2% in post-market trading.

feed.title: Parker to Announce Fiscal 2026 Second Quarter Earnings on
feed.summary: Parker Hannifin Corporation will release its fiscal 2026 second-quarter earnings on January 29, 2026, before the market opens. This will be followed by a conference call and webcast at 11:00 a.m. Eastern time to discuss the results and address questions from investors and analysts. The webcast will be available on Parker's investor website, with an archived replay accessible later the same day.

feed.title: Has The Market Overlooked Pfizer (PFE) After The Post‑Pandemic Reset?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Pfizer's valuation, concluding that the market may have overlooked its intrinsic value. Using a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model and Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio analysis, Simply Wall St suggests Pfizer (PFE) is significantly undervalued. The article also presents "Narratives" with bull and bear cases to explain differing valuation perspectives on the company's future prospects.

feed.title: Unveiling BankUnited (BKU) Q4 Outlook: Wall Street Estimates for Key Metrics
feed.summary: Wall Street analysts project BankUnited (BKU) to report Q4 earnings of $0.84 per share, a 7.7% year-over-year decline, and revenues of $277.83 million, up 5.1%. Key metrics forecasts include average interest-earning assets at $34.13 billion and total non-performing loans at $376.50 million. The stock currently holds a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell), indicating an expectation to underperform the market.

feed.title: Seaport Highlights LVS Strength in Singapore Amid Macau Cost Challenges
feed.summary: Seaport Research Partners highlights Las Vegas Sands Corp (LVS) strong performance in Singapore through its Marina Bay Sands property, which is outperforming expectations and dominating its market. This success provides a counterbalance to the rising operational costs and competitive pressures faced by LVS and other gaming companies in Macau. The report also details the varying performances of major Macau operators like Galaxy Entertainment, Melco Resorts, MGM China, and SJM Holdings, underscoring the importance of geographic diversification and strategic positioning in Asia's gaming industry.

feed.title: FDA Grants Orphan Drug Status to Atossa Therapeutics' (ATOS) Z-E
feed.summary: Atossa Therapeutics (ATOS) has received Orphan Drug Designation from the FDA for its drug candidate, Z-endoxifen, for treating Duchenne muscular dystrophy. This designation is a significant step for the clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, which focuses on unmet medical needs in oncology. Despite facing challenges in revenue growth and profitability, Atossa demonstrates strong financial health with no debt and high liquidity, suggesting resilience against potential setbacks.

feed.title: BriaCell Therapeutics Announces Closing of US$30 million Public Offering
feed.summary: BriaCell Therapeutics Corp., a clinical-stage biotechnology company, announced the closing of a public offering that generated approximately US$30 million in gross proceeds. The offering included 5,366,726 units, each consisting of one common share (or pre-funded warrant) and one warrant, sold at US$5.59 per unit. The net proceeds will be used for working capital, general corporate purposes, and advancing the company's business objectives.

feed.title: AMTD Group Initiates Legal Action Against Former Employee for Serious Defamation
feed.summary: AMTD IDEA Group has filed a lawsuit against former employee Benjamin Eymere for serious defamation and malicious harassment of staff to protect its brand reputation. Eymere's termination was due to management errors and misconduct, and his appeals in Paris courts have been repeatedly rejected. AMTD also asserts that L'Officiel, acquired in 2022, is financially strong and debt-free.

feed.title: Customer-Funded 1.4 GW Data Center Deal Could Be A Game Changer For DTE Energy (DTE)
feed.summary: DTE Energy recently announced a fully customer-funded 1.4 GW data center power agreement, which is poised to benefit existing customers and reshape the company's investment narrative. Despite regulatory scrutiny from Michigan's Attorney General regarding the fast-track approval of data center contracts, DTE reaffirmed its 2025 EPS guidance and 2026 outlook, signaling confidence in its earnings trajectory. This development highlights both significant growth opportunities from data center demand and the ongoing risk of regulatory challenges.

feed.title: Ross, CEO of Trio Petroleum, sells $33k in shares
feed.summary: Trio Petroleum Corp's CEO, Robin A. Ross, sold 37,500 shares of common stock for approximately $33,352.50 to cover tax obligations related to restricted stock issued in 2025. Following this transaction, Ross directly owns 737,500 shares. The company also recently entered an at-the-market equity agreement with Ladenburg Thalmann & Co. Inc. to sell up to $3.6 million in common stock.

feed.title: B. Riley Adjusts Price Target on Amkor Technology to $48 From $29, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: B. Riley has adjusted its price target for Amkor Technology (AMKR) to $48 from $29, while maintaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This update reflects a significant increase in the price target, signaling a revised outlook for the semiconductor packaging and test services provider.
